pokemon:electabuzz.name: Electabuzz
pokemon:electabuzz.summary: When a storm arrives, gangs of this Pokémon compete with each other to scale heights that are likely to be stricken by lightning bolts. Some towns use Electabuzz in place of lightning rods.
expansionNotes: Competitive, encounter, and media coverage can be expanded later without changing the page contract.

pokedexEntries.text: When a storm arrives, gangs of this Pokémon compete with each other to scale heights that are likely to be stricken by lightning bolts. Some towns use Electabuzz in place of lightning rods.
It windmills its arms to slightly boost its punches. Foes have been known to escape in the meantime.
Research is progressing on storing lightning in Electabuzz so this energy can be used at any time.
Normally found near power plants, they can wander away and cause major blackouts in cities.
When two ELECTABUZZ touch, they control the electric currents to communicate their feelings.
Half of all blackouts occur when this Pokémon appears at power plants and eats electricity.
When a storm approaches, it competes with others to scale heights that are likely to be stricken by lightning. Some towns use ELECTABUZZ in place of lightning rods.
It loves to feed on strong electricity. It occasionally appears around large power plants and so on.
Electricity runs across the surface of its body. In darkness, its en­ tire body glows a whitish-blue.
Electricity runs across the surface of its body. In darkness, its entire body glows a whitish-blue.
Normally found near power plants, they can wander away and cause major blackouts in cities.
Feeds on electrical energy. During sudden showers beneath looming thunderclouds, one can observe Electabuzz scaling tall trees, where the Pokémon will then wait for lightning to strike.
If a major power outage occurs, it is certain that this Pokémon has eaten electricity at a power plant.
If a major power outage occurs, it is certain that this Pokémon has eaten electricity at a power plant.
Electricity leaks from it in amounts far greater than the amount of electricity it eats.
When a storm arrives, gangs of this Pokémon compete with each other to scale heights that are likely to be stricken by lightning bolts. Some towns use Electabuzz in place of lightning rods.
Half of all blackouts occur when this Pokémon appears at power plants and eats electricity.
It windmills its arms to slightly boost its punches. Foes have been known to escape in the meantime.
Normally found near power plants, they can wander away and cause major blackouts in cities.
When a storm arrives, gangs of this POKéMON compete with each other to scale heights that are likely to be stricken by lightning bolts. Some towns use ELECTABUZZ in place of lightning rods.
When a storm arrives, gangs of this POKéMON compete with each other to scale heights that are likely to be stricken by lightning bolts. Some towns use ELECTABUZZ in place of lightning rods.
With the coming of a storm, many of these Pokémon will gather under tall trees and sit there waiting for lightning to strike.
Its body constant­ ly discharges electricity. Get­ ting close to it will make your hair stand on end.
Its body constantly discharges electricity. Getting close to it will make your hair stand on end.
Half of all sudden blackouts are caused by Electabuzz gathering at electric power plants and gobbling up electricity.
Many power plants keep Ground-type Pokémon around as a defense against Electabuzz that come seeking electricity.
While it’s often blamed for power outages, the truth is the cause of outages is more often an error on the part of the electric company.
Electricity permeates its body. It swings its arms round and round to charge up electricity before unleashing a punch.
It windmills its arms to slightly boost its punches. Foes have been known to escape in the meantime.
Research is progressing on storing lightning in Electabuzz so this energy can be used at any time.
Electricity runs across the surface of its body. In darkness, its entire body glows a whitish blue.
It loves to feed on strong electricity. It occasionally appears around large power plants and so on.
If a major power outage occurs, it is certain that this POKéMON has eaten electricity at a power plant.

competitiveSummary: Competitive notes are pending a richer source import.
loreSummary: When a storm arrives, gangs of this Pokémon compete with each other to scale heights that are likely to be stricken by lightning bolts. Some towns use Electabuzz in place of lightning rods.

pokemon:elekid.name: Elekid
pokemon:elekid.summary: Elekid stores electricity in its body. If it touches metal and accidentally discharges all its built-up electricity, this Pokémon begins swinging its arms in circles to recharge itself.
expansionNotes: Competitive, encounter, and media coverage can be expanded later without changing the page contract.

pokedexEntries.text: Elekid stores electricity in its body. If it touches metal and accidentally discharges all its built-up electricity, this Pokémon begins swinging its arms in circles to recharge itself.
A weak electric current flows between its horns. Sticking a hand there shocks the unwary.
Spinning its arms around to generate electricity makes the area between its horns shine light blue.
It loves violent thunder. The space between its horns flickers bluish- white when it is charging energy.
It generates electricity by whirling its arms. However, it can’t store the energy it makes.
If it touches metal and discharges the electricity it has stored in its body, an ELEKID begins swinging its arms in circles to recharge itself.
Even in the most vicious storm, this POKéMON plays happily if thunder rumbles in the sky.
It rotates its arms to generate electricity, but it tires easily, so it charges up only a little bit.
It rotates its arms to generate electricity, but it tires easily, so it only charges up a little bit.
It rotates its arms to generate electricity, but it tires easily, so it charges up only a little bit.
They generate electricity by spinning their arms. During a thunderstorm, if one hears the lively voices of children out in the wilderness, what one is actually hearing are the voices of Elekid.
This Pokémon is constantly fighting with Togedemaru that try to steal its electricity. It’s a pretty even match.
Elekid stores electricity in its body. If it touches metal and accidentally discharges all its built-up electricity, this Pokémon begins swinging its arms in circles to recharge itself.
It generates electricity by whirling its arms. However, it can’t store the energy it makes.
A weak electric current flows between its horns. Sticking a hand there shocks the unwary.
ELEKID stores electricity in its body. If it touches metal and accidentally discharges all its built-up electricity, this POKéMON begins swinging its arms in circles to recharge itself.
ELEKID stores electricity in its body. If it touches metal and accidentally discharges all its built-up electricity, this POKéMON begins swinging its arms in circles to recharge itself.
It’s not good at storing electricity yet. This Pokémon sneaks into people’s homes, looking for electrical outlets to eat electricity from.
Even in the most vicious storm, this POKéMON plays happily if thunder rumbles in the sky.
Even in the most vicious storm, this Pokémon plays happily if thunder rumbles in the sky.
When it’s in a house, electrical outlets serve as its baby bottles. It sucks down electricity.
When a storm approaches, this Pokémon gets restless. Once Elekid hears the sound of thunder, it gets full-on rowdy.
When its horns shine a bluish white, that’s the sign it’s fully charged. You’ll get a shocking jolt if you touch it!
When it hears the crash of thunder, Elekid’s mood improves. It can be useful to record that sound and play it when Elekid’s feeling down.
A weak electric current flows between its horns. Sticking a hand there shocks the unwary.
Spinning its arms around to generate electricity makes the area between its horns shine light blue.
It generates electricity by whirling its arms. However, it can’t store the energy it makes.
Spinning its arms around to generate electricity makes the area between its horns shine light blue.

loreSummary: Elekid stores electricity in its body. If it touches metal and accidentally discharges all its built-up electricity, this Pokémon begins swinging its arms in circles to recharge itself.

pokemon:electivire.name: Electivire
pokemon:electivire.summary: It pushes the tips of its two tails against the foe, then lets loose with over 20,000 volts of power.
expansionNotes: Competitive, encounter, and media coverage can be expanded later without changing the page contract.

pokedexEntries.text: It pushes the tips of its two tails against the foe, then lets loose with over 20,000 volts of power.
Heedless of enemy attacks, it closes in, shoves its tails onto the foe, then looses high voltage.
The instant it presses the tips of its tails onto an opponent, it sends over 20,000 volts of electricity into the foe.
It pushes the tips of its two tails against the foe, then lets loose with over 20,000 volts of power.
As its electric charge amplifies, blue sparks begin to crackle between its horns.
Its evolution was induced by an unusual item, and its electrical output rises along with its heart rate. From its tails, it can unleash an electric current measuring 20,000 volts.
When it gets excited, it thumps its chest. With every thud, thunder roars and electric sparks shower all around.
As its electric charge amplifies, blue sparks begin to crackle between its horns.
It pushes the tips of its two tails against the foe, then lets loose with over 20,000 volts of power.
Heedless of enemy attacks, it closes in, shoves its tails onto the foe, then looses high voltage.
In terms of electrical-energy output, Electivire is one of the best among all Electric Pokémon. It discharges high-voltage currents from its tails.
As its electric charge amplifies, blue sparks begin to crackle between its horns.
It pushes the tips of its tails against its foes and then lets loose a high-voltage current. Its foes are burned to a crisp in an instant.
The amount of electrical energy this Pokémon produces is proportional to the rate of its pulse. The voltage jumps while Electivire is battling.
A single Electivire can provide enough electricity for all the buildings in a big city for a year.
It grips its tail, which spews electricity, and then beats down opponents with the power of its electrified fist.
Heedless of enemy attacks, it closes in, shoves its tails onto the foe, then looses high voltage.
The instant it presses the tips of its tails onto an opponent, it sends over 20,000 volts of electricity into the foe.
As its electric charge amplifies, blue sparks begin to crackle between its horns.
It pushes the tips of its two tails against the foe, then lets loose with over 20,000 volts of power.

competitiveSummary: Competitive notes are pending a richer source import.
loreSummary: It pushes the tips of its two tails against the foe, then lets loose with over 20,000 volts of power.
